cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking how to save money when visiting Disney World based on the passage provided. The options are A to D. First, I need to recall what the passage said. The passage mentioned several tips. Let me break it down. It says that peak seasons are more costly for accommodations, both inside and outside the theme park. So avoiding peak times might save money, but option A is about going in peak seasons, which would be the opposite. So A is probably not correct. Then, it says staying outside the resort can save money if you stay for a week or two. That's option B. That seems right. The passage says that staying outside is a choice that may save a lot of money for a week or two. So B is a possible correct answer. Next, the passage mentions buying food ahead of time. It says you can have meals at the hotel or cheaper restaurants instead of buying in Disney World. So option C says buying food ahead of time in Disney World. Wait, no—the passage says to avoid buying food in Disney World. So buying ahead of time would mean not buying there. So maybe option C is incorrect because it says "in Disney World," which is the opposite of the advice. So C is wrong. Then, option D is carrying enough food and drinks. The passage says carrying food and beverages and enough water would help reduce costs. So D is correct as well. So both B and D are mentioned as ways to save money. But the question is asking which of the options is correct according to the passage. Looking at the options again: A: Peak seasons. The passage says peak seasons are costly, so A is wrong.
